prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a long winding road taken by a small adventuring robot is being read aloud like a sentence by a friendly scribe, the road curling neatly into the shape of a flowing line of connected beads, each bead a colored pebble for a moment of the journey, the scribe holding a quill and following the beads left to right with a delighted expression. Generous negative space, one clear visual gag of a path becoming a readable line. Style friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces, minimal clutter. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot's winding journey curls into a flowing string of colored beads while a scribe reads it left to right like a sentence.
caption: Treat an agent's trajectory not as evidence to optimize but as a sentence to be modeled, and a sequence model becomes a policy.

after_heading: 2. Why It Works, and Why It Is Appealing
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: split scene, on the left a weary hiker robot endlessly climbing a foggy shifting mountain whose peak keeps moving, on the right a calm robot at a serene counter simply pointing at the height it wants on a smooth glowing slope and being lifted there at once. The contrast between exhausting climb and effortless request is the gag. Generous negative space. Style friendly like a Kurzgesagt panel, expressive body language, minimal clutter. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Left, a robot exhausts itself chasing a peak that keeps moving; right, a calm robot simply points to a desired height and is lifted there.
caption: Classical control optimizes by searching for the best action, while sequence modeling just conditions on the outcome you want and reads off behavior consistent with it.

prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a friendly genie rising from a lamp happily granting a modest sensible wish to a small robot, but when the robot greedily gestures at an absurdly enormous pile of riches far beyond anything seen, the genie shrugs apologetically and produces only a tiny puff of confetti, having no memory of a wish that big. The gag is a genie bounded by lived experience. Generous negative space. Style friendly like a clever XKCD panel, expressive faces. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A genie cheerfully grants a modest wish but can only shrug and produce a tiny puff when a robot demands riches grander than anything it has ever seen.
caption: Return conditioning is a literal-minded genie: ask for an outcome the data has delivered and it obliges, ask for far more and it returns confident nonsense.

after_heading: 2. Hindsight Relabeling: Turning Failure Into Success
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: an archer robot fires an arrow at a distant target but misses badly, the arrow thunking into a tree far to the side, then the robot cheerfully walks over and paints a fresh bullseye ring around exactly where the arrow landed, beaming with pride at a perfect hit. The gag is moving the goal to the outcome rather than the outcome to the goal. Generous negative space. Style friendly like a Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot archer misses the target, then paints a new bullseye around wherever the arrow actually landed and celebrates a perfect hit.
caption: Hindsight relabeling reassigns the goal to whatever a trajectory actually reached, so every failed attempt becomes a textbook success at something.

after_heading: 3. The Limit of Return-Conditioning: It Cannot Extrapolate
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: two separate stretches of bridge each cross half of a wide ravine but stop short in the middle with a gap between them, a hopeful robot stands at the start wanting to cross the whole way but cannot join the two halves, while off to the side a clever spider effortlessly spins a single connecting thread between them. The gag is the inability to combine two good halves into one good whole. Generous negative space. Style friendly like a clever XKCD panel, expressive faces. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Two half bridges stop short with a gap between them while a robot cannot join them, though a spider easily spins a thread connecting both halves.
caption: A return conditioned policy cannot stitch two good partial paths into a better whole it never saw, which is exactly the gap value based methods bridge.

after_heading: 2. Why This Matters: Forecasting and Decision Making Are One Problem
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a friendly robot peering into a crystal ball that shows a branching road of possible futures, the robot calmly reaching in and plucking the single branch it prefers, the act of foreseeing and the act of choosing happening in one smooth motion. The gag is that predicting and deciding are the same gesture. Generous negative space. Style friendly like a Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face, minimal clutter. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot gazes into a crystal ball showing branching future roads and plucks its preferred branch, foreseeing and choosing in one motion.
caption: To decide is to forecast the consequences of candidate actions and pick the best, so a good forecaster already contains a policy.
